title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Browser and Node Client Syst… · Compiler Case: Fault Injection
summary: A cross-runtime SDK needs deterministic fetch fakes, local integration tests, abort and truncation faults, contract fixtures, and Web Performance telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts fault injection increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject cancellation, timeout, reset, partial body, malformed fields, bad media type, redirect, and retry exhaustion deterministically. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Fault Injection · Theory Fault Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model test T-3 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Inject cancellation, timeout, reset, partial body, malformed fields, bad media type, redirect, and retry exhaustion deterministically. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fault Injection contract for the supplied data.

For Fetch and TypeScript Predict Fault Injection · Theory Fault Injection, keep Fault Injection connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
